exemplar.title: To what extent did Unilever Sustainable Living Plan (USLP) contribute to its competitive advantage in the FMCG market in terms of customer loyalty and operational efficiency?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent did Unilever Sustainable Living Plan (USLP) contribute to its competitive advantage in the FMCG market in terms of customer loyalty and operational efficiency?

subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: Topic is communicated accurately and effectively with comprehensive background and clear significance of the ten-year scope
Research question is clearly stated, well-focused and consistently connected to the discussion via measurable dimensions
Methodology tools are appropriately chosen and justified, showing informed selection aligned to each dimension of the question
weaknesses: Investigation relies solely on secondary sources with no plan to screen for credibility or triangulate data
Minor repetition of generic definitions in the introduction slightly dilutes focus
Some figures (e.g. SDG icons) are visually overloaded and could be simplified for clarity
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The submission meets the descriptor for a 5â6 band overall: the topic is introduced and explained comprehensively, and the research question is well-focused and integrated. Methodological justification is strong, but the lack of critical screening of secondary sources prevents full marks. Strand scores of 5, 5 and 4 average to a solid performance warranting 5/6.

strengths: Demonstrates clear and coherent subject knowledge of strategic frameworks and financial metrics
Applies source material in mostly relevant and appropriate ways, weaving data points into the narrative
Terminology is accurate and consistently applied, including discipline-specific vocabulary (e.g. VRIO, operating ratio)
weaknesses: Occasional unsourced cause-and-effect assertions (e.g. direct link between USLP and revenue growth) lack full substantiation
Use of market forecasts beyond the 2010â2020 window slightly undermines period relevance
Inclusion of a generic SWOT diagram adds limited bespoke insight compared to a custom analysis
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments of 4, 5 and 5 indicate strong performance in Knowledge and Understanding, with clear mastery of concepts and mostly appropriate use of sources. Minor lapses in rigor and focus prevent the top mark but overall justify a 5/6.

strengths: Good breadth of research clearly relevant to the research question, with systematic organization of multi-year financial and industry data
Analysis moves beyond description, benchmarking ratios and linking qualitative SWOT insights to quantitative evidence
Discussion offers a coherent, balanced argument, acknowledges tool limitations and suggests avenues for further study
weaknesses: Exclusive reliance on secondary data with no critique of source bias limits depth of evaluation
Competitor analysis lacks comparable quantitative metrics to deepen the competitive context
Some data extend beyond the study period and are not fully aligned to the 2010â2020 scope
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: With strand scores of 8, 7 and 7, the investigation displays good to strong critical thinking: clear analysis and well-supported conclusions. However, absence of primary data, limited source critique and only partial critical evaluation preclude entry to the highest band. An 8/12 reflects the overall strong but not exceptional critical engagement.

strengths: Structure follows IB conventions (title page, contents, numbered sections, conclusion, references) and aids navigation
Logical headings and sub-sections facilitate reader orientation
Visuals are embedded near relevant discussion points, enhancing readability
weaknesses: Inconsistent citation formatting and a non-alphabetized Works Cited list reduce clarity
Several figures lack explicit source citations or clear captions
Occasional missing page numbers and non-standard TOC indentation hinder smooth navigation
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Structure fully meets the top band descriptor, but layout and formatting inconsistencies (citations, figure sources, pagination) prevent highest marks. This places the work solidly in the mid to upper range of the presentation criterion at 3/4.

strandSelections.reasoning:
The research question is explicitly stated, employs a clear evaluative command term ("To what extent"), identifies two measurable dimensions of advantage (customer loyalty and operational efficiency) and anchors the investigation within 2010-2020.  Subsequent sections return consistently to these two dimensions, confirming a strong connection between question and discussion.

Although the double focus adds breadth, the tools selected (SWOT, operating ratio, VRIO) align logically with each element, showing a purposeful narrowing of scope.  This satisfies the rubricâs expectation of a well-focused, effectively connected question suitable for the 5-6 band.

The discussion is coherent, drawing explicit links back to the research question and acknowledging tool limitations, costâbenefit tensions and data constraints.  The conclusion offers a balanced âmedium extentâ judgement and suggests avenues for further research, indicating some critical reflection.

Evaluation remains partial: alternative interpretations (e.g., wider financial ratios, macro-economic shocks) are noted but not deeply explored.  This aligns with a âwell-developed argument with partially critical evaluation,â meriting 7 in the 7-9 band.

The student demonstrates clear, coherent understanding of strategic frameworks (SWOT, VRIO) and financial metrics, integrating syllabus and extra-syllabus concepts (operating ratio) accurately.  Explanations of each toolâs purpose and limitations show maturity of subject knowledge.

Occasional oversimplifications (equating a lower operating ratio directly with âoverall efficiencyâ) are present but do not undermine the overall strength of knowledge displayed.  Effective synthesis across qualitative and quantitative perspectives justifies a placement in the 5-6 band, with a score of 5.

Terminology such as âoperational efficiency,â âvirgin plastic,â âvalue, rarity, imitability,â and âstakeholder relationshipsâ is defined and then applied correctly throughout the essay.  Tool-specific vocabulary (e.g., operating expenses, COGS, net sales) is employed with precision in formula explanations and data interpretation.

Inconsistencies are minimal; therefore the use is accurate and consistent, fulfilling the criteria for the 5-6 band.

Analytical sections move beyond description by benchmarking operating ratios, comparing VRIO elements with competitor initiatives and linking SWOT observations to quantitative evidence.  Conclusions (e.g., USLPâs limited effect on operating ratio but stronger impact on loyalty) are logical and usually supported by data.

However, the analysis stops short of sophisticated econometric proof; some causal claims are asserted, and counter-arguments receive cursory treatment.  The work therefore fits âclear analysis with well-supported conclusions,â earning a 7 within the 7-9 band.

Evidence from financial statements, industry surveys and company reports is closely tied to the SWOT, operating-ratio table and VRIO discussion, demonstrating mostly relevant application.  Figures are presented clearly through tables and graphs, and specific data points (e.g., Unileverâs â¬1 bn savings, operating-ratio values) are woven into the narrative.

Yet, certain assertions (e.g., cause-and-effect links between USLP and revenue growth) are not fully substantiated, and some sources of lower academic rigour are cited without critique.  This points to âmostlyâ rather than âconsistentlyâ appropriate use, meriting the 3-4 band with a score of 4.

The essay draws on a generally relevant range of secondary sourcesâannual reports, industry databases, practitioner articles and news outletsâand applies three analytical tools (SWOT, operating ratio benchmarking and VRIO).  The student justifies each toolâs choice and explains calculation methods, indicating informed selection.

However, the investigation remains wholly secondary; there is little critical screening of source credibility and no triangulation through primary data or academic literature.  Some data (e.g., LinkedIn blogs) are used uncritically.  The range is therefore adequate but not fully âappropriateâ or âeffectively selected,â placing the work solidly in the 3-4 band rather than the top band.

The student assembles multi-year financial data, industry growth forecasts and competitor sustainability initiatives that are clearly germane to the question, evidencing âgoodâ breadth and relevance.  Data are sourced from reputable databases (Statista, Macrotrends), company reports and PwC surveys, and are systematically organised into tables and visuals.

Nevertheless, exclusive reliance on secondary material and limited critique of source reliability constrain the investigation from âexcellent.â  This positions the strand in the upper-middle (7-9) bracket, appropriately at 8.

The layout generally adheres to EE formatting expectations (margins, font, spacing), and a word count is provided.  However, inconsistent citation style, occasional absent page numbers and several figures lacking immediate source attributions indicate that not all layout conventions are correctly applied.

Because someâbut not allârequirements are met, the strand aligns with the 1-2 band; the stronger end of that range (2) reflects partial but incomplete compliance.

The topicâUnileverâs USLP and its contribution to competitive advantageâis presented immediately, is framed within wider sustainability trends and the FMCG context, and is reinforced through clear background data and definitions.  The student explains why the theme is significant and situates the ten-year time frame, demonstrating an assured grasp of scope and relevance.

Minor shortcomings (occasional repetition in the introduction and a brief diversion into generic definitions) do not materially affect clarity.  Overall, the explanation is comprehensive, precise and engaging, fully meeting the âaccurately and effectively communicatedâ descriptor for the 5-6 band.

The essay follows IB EE conventionsâtitle page, contents, numbered sections, visuals, conclusion and works citedâthereby meeting structural requirements.  Headings are logical and facilitate navigation; visuals are embedded near discussion points.

While some page numbers and figure captions are missing or inconsistently formatted, the overall structure remains clearly appropriate, placing the work in the top (3-4) band for this strand.

annotations.comment: The Works Cited list uses inconsistent citation formats and is not alphabetized; applying a single citation style and ordering entries alphabetically would improve presentation.
The conclusion suggests future research but lacks personal reflection on the research process; adding an evaluative reflection would meet engagement criteria more fully.

While introducing the operating ratio and VRIO, there is no acknowledgment of the limitations inherent in a wholly secondary data approach; a brief critique of source bias would improve critical depth.
The overview cites FMCG market forecasts to 2031, which extends beyond the 2010â2020 period under study; focusing only on data within the research timeframe would strengthen relevance.
Several SWOT items cite low-rigour sources (e.g., LinkedIn blogs); replacing these with peer-reviewed or industry-standard references would strengthen source credibility.
The analysis concludes no discernible USLP impact on operating ratio without any statistical test; incorporating basic significance testing or trend analysis would substantiate the claim.

Figure 7âs trend in global sustainability reporting is informative but is not explicitly linked to Unileverâs rarity evaluation; clarify how Unileverâs disclosures compare to the N100/G250 cohorts.
The VRIO section lacks distinct subheadings for Value, Rarity, Imitability, and Organization; adding these subsections would improve structural clarity in presentation.
When claiming â¬1 billion in USLP-driven savings, no competitor benchmarks are provided; comparative figures would contextualize the relative value of these efficiencies.
The inclusion of a word count and session details on the title page meets IB formatting requirements and evidences attention to presentation conventions.
The introduction repeats a broad definition of sustainability that could be condensed; focusing more quickly on Unileverâs specific context would strengthen subject relevance and coherence.

The title page clearly presents the research question and situates the study within the context of Unileverâs sustainability efforts, demonstrating a strong focus and method by immediately orienting the reader to the investigationâs scope.
The table of contents uses non-standard spacing for sub-entries (e.g., SWOT, operating ratio) which hinders readability; consistent indentation and dot leaders would improve navigation.
The methodology section defines chosen tools but lacks any plan for critically screening source credibility; discussing evaluation criteria for secondary data would enhance methodological rigor.
Competitor profiles mention P&G and NestlÃ© but omit quantitative metrics on their sustainability performance; including comparable figures would deepen the competitive context.
Figure 1 lacks a clear source citation in the caption; explicitly referencing the data origin would ensure academic integrity and improve presentation.
The SDG icons figure is visually overloaded; a simplified table summarizing key USLP achievements could communicate progress more effectively.
Linking the revenue chartâs upward trend to USLP is presented without controlling for other factors; a clearer discussion of correlation versus causation is needed.
Including a generic SWOT diagram from an external source adds little bespoke value; directly presenting the candidateâs customized SWOT would be more purposeful.
The operating ratio formula is embedded as a MathML block; converting it to LaTeX (e.g., $$\frac{Operating\ Expenses + COGS}{Net\ Sales}$$) would align with mathematical notation standards.
The operating ratio table extends data through 2023, but the research question focuses on 2010â2020; restricting the dataset to the study period would improve methodological focus.
Figure 5âs caption lacks explicit axis labels and a data source; annotating both axes and citing the data origin enhance clarity and replicability.

The imitability discussion notes social complexity but lacks concrete cases of competitors attempting and failing to replicate these initiatives; adding real-world examples would strengthen the argument.
The organization dimension claims effective USLP integration but omits evidence of internal structures (e.g., KPIs, governance) that operationalize sustainability; include process details.
Conclusions about customer loyalty are asserted without primary consumer data or survey evidence; integrating direct stakeholder feedback would substantiate loyalty claims.
